configurePresets.name: dev
configurePresets.description: Fast iteration: Ninja scheduling, ccache object reuse, no -O (compile-contract/behavior suites need neither optimization nor NDEBUG). Header isolation is OFF; use the `ci` preset for the full one-TU-per-header contract.

configurePresets.name: ci
configurePresets.displayName: CI (mirrors the GitHub Actions cpp job)
configurePresets.description: Local mirror of the GitHub Actions C++ gates: header isolation ON as one object target with one TU per public header, X3D_CPP_WERROR=ON (warnings become errors, the merge gate). Use `mise run build-ci` to drive the same gate locally before pushing.

configurePresets.name: san
configurePresets.description: Debug Address + UndefinedBehavior sanitizer build for the behavior suite. Same flags and target set the GitHub Actions `cpp-san` job uses. WERROR is OFF — the ci preset is the warning-enforcement gate; this one is purely for memory bugs. Per-header isolation and other compile-only/opt-in targets are excluded because instrumenting code that never executes adds no runtime bug-finding and dominated the job's wall-clock.

configurePresets.name: fuzz
configurePresets.description: libFuzzer harness for parseDocument (Clang-only — `-fsanitize=fuzzer`). Build with `cmake --preset fuzz && cmake --build build-fuzz --target x3d_parse_fuzz`. Run `./build-fuzz/x3d_parse_fuzz corpus/` to explore, or `--max_total_time=60` for a bounded CI smoke. WERROR is OFF — Clang has additional default warnings (e.g. -Wmissing-braces in the generated code) that don't reflect actual bugs and would block the fuzz gate.
